def: part. Engendered, begotten, born, produced, occasioned.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَنَّت (jannat), borrowed from Arabic جَنَّة (janna). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi جنت (jnt /⁠janat⁠/).

def: s.m. A father, &c. = janitā.
ex:
دُکھوں کی گِنْتِی سے کالی ہَے زِنْدَگی کی کِتاب
تو جَنْتَری میں اُنْھیں سال و ماہ لِکھ لِیجے
dukhõ kī gintī se kālī hai zindagī kī kitāb
to jantarī mẽ unhẽ sāl o māh likh līje

The book of life is dark from the counting of sorrows
So write them in the calendar by year and month

def: s.f. Progenitrix, mother.
ex:
دُکھوں کی گِنْتِی سے کالی ہَے زِنْدَگی کی کِتاب
تو جَنْتَری میں اُنْھیں سال و ماہ لِکھ لِیجے
dukhõ kī gintī se kālī hai zindagī kī kitāb
to jantarī mẽ unhẽ sāl o māh likh līje

The book of life is dark from the counting of sorrows
So write them in the calendar by year and month

def: s.m. Entanglements, embarrassments, troubles, cares or anxieties (of wordly concerns or engagements); trouble, complication, bother, fuss; a teasing, tiring, or troublesome person or business; an encumbrance, a clog, load; a plague, pest, bore
subs: (-ke), To get into an entanglement, fall into difficulties; to get into a mess.

def: s.m. A large musket, a swivel ( = jazāʼil, q.v.). s.m. A large musket or rifle (used with a prong or rest); a wall-piece; a swivel
subs: s.m. One who fires a wall-piece or swivel.

def: adj. & s.m. Occasioning trouble, embarrassing, troublesome, &c.; — a troublesome person; one who molests.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. jān, 'knowledge.' adj. (met.) Dear, beloved
subs: s.m. Animator, creator of life (an epithet of the Deity)
jān-bāz, adj. & s.m. Sporting with life, risking life, venturesome, daring, intrepid, spirited, mettlesome; — one who sports with life, a daring or intrepid man, &c.

s.f. The state of life being at stake (e.g. jān-bāzī-ke waqt, 'when life is at stake'); risking life (in battle, &c.); intrepidity, mettle, spiritedness; working (at anything) as if life depended on success
To risk life (in battle, or an enterprise, &c.); to act bravely or fearlessly, be intrepid, &c.
(-kī, or apnī), To save the life (of); to shirk, skulk; to funk
'The soul to be surrendered to God'; to die, expire
adj. & s.m. Animating, life-giving; forgiving; — forgiver of a capital crime; pardoner
s.f. Giving life, vivification; sparing life; forgiveness, pardon (of a capital crime); salvation
To give up the ghost, to expire
adj. At the point of death, dying, expiring
(-par), To feel life to be a burden, to be weary of life
v.n. To be exposed to imminent danger
v.n. To be in danger of life
(or -pa) khelnā, v.n. To put life at stake, to risk life, to run into danger, to place oneself in a perilous position; to sport or trifle with (one's) life
(-meṅ), To receive the breath of life, to be quickened; to receive new life, be refreshed, to revive
s.f. Risk or danger of life; — adv. In danger
jān ćhupānā (apnī), To skrink (from), escape (from), to skulk, shirk ( = jān baćānā)
(-kī, or apnī), To escape with life; to get rid of
adj. Having life or strength; powerful, active; lively, spirited (as a horse); — s.m. A living thing, an animal (generally implying strength); — jān-dārī, s.f. Animation, spirit, spiritedness
(-par), To yield up life; to lose or sacrifice (one's) life (for), to die (for)
(or supār), adj. Resigning (one's) life into the hands of another (an epithet of a lover)
adj. Life destroying, destructive of life (as poison, or a snake, &c.)
s.f. The taking or destruction of life
adj. Life-consuming, soul-tormenting, very painful; heart-inflaming; beloved; — jān-se jānā, v.n. To give up life, to pass away, to die
v.t. To deprive of life, to kill
v.n. To wash one's hands of life, to be weary of life; to despair of life

adj. Ready to sacrifice oneself for another, devoted, zealous, fervent; — jān-fishānī, s.f. Sacrificing (one's) life in the service of another, devotion, devotedness, zeal; hard labour; extreme diligence; — jān-fishānī karnā, v.n. To be very devoted or zealous; to practise extreme diligence; to labour or strive hard, to strain every nerve
jān-kā lāgū, jān-kā lewā, s.m. Deadly foe, mortal enemy; — jān-kā lāgū honā (-kī), To pursue (one) to death; to persecute, harass, worry
adj. Life-reducing, soul-exhausting; heart-breaking, afflicting; affecting, pathetic; — jān-kāhī, s.f. Diminution of life; what tends to shorten life
s.m., jān-kandanī, s.f., jān-kanī, s.f. The agonies of death, agony, torture
(-kī), To reduce to straits, to overcome, exhaust; to worry, tease, vex, annoy, plague, torment
(-ke sabab), To sacrifice or give up life (for)
s.f. Safety of (one's) life; life, quarter
(-ko), To hold (one) as dear as life, to deem precious, to value or prize highly
adj. Soul-melting, life-dissolving, life-destroying; baneful, pernicious; weakening; consuming; — jān-gudāzī, s.f. Dissolving or taking away of life; banefulness, &c.
(-kī), To destroy life, to kill; to worry, tease, &c. ( = jān khānā); to exert oneself to the utmost, strain every nerve
(-kī, merī), To be revived ( = jān paṛnā); to be satisfied, be comforted
adj. Scattering life; ready to sacrifice life (for another), devoted; dying in agony; — jān-nis̤ārī, s.f. Readiness to sacrifice life (for another), devotedness, devotion
v.n. Life to depart, to die
s.f. lit. 'Clinging to life'; a sudden reviving of consciousness and life in a dying man previous to his giving up the ghost (caused by the weeping and lamentation of those around him)
v.n. To make life wearisome; to worry; to work to death (used by women)
adj. Lifeless, dead; dispirited; listless; weak, feeble, inactive.

def: adv. = jān-ke, and jān-būjh-ke, qq.v. adv. Knowing, wittingly, &c. = jān-būjh-ke, q.v.
subs: v.n. To pretend or feign ignorance.

def: s.m. = jākaṛ, q.v.
adv. Knowing, wittingly, &c. = jān-būjh-ke, q.v.
subs: v.n. To pretend or feign ignorance.

def: s.m. Birth, production, origin; nativity; existence, life; state of existence; life-time
subs: vulg. janam-ashṭamī (S. janma+ash˚), s.f. The eighth day in the dark half of the month Sāvan-Bhādoṅ, the birth-day of Kr̤ishṇa
(janma+an˚), s.m. Another birth; another state of existence; the future life; regeneration
adj. Belonging to or done in another life; of or pertaining to another state of existence
vulg. janam-andhā, adj. & s.m. Blind from birth; — one born blind
(apnā), To waste (one's) life; to indulge in sensual and sinful pleasures
To be ruined for life; to become a widow
s.m. & adv. A whole life, a life-time; — through life, for a life-time
janm-bhūm, janm-bhūmī, s.f. Birth-place, native land, fatherland

janam-pattrā, s.m. = janam-pattrī, s.f. Horoscope, astrological record of nativity, the paper or scroll on which are recorded the year, lunar day, configuration, and relative position of the planets, &c. of the birth of a particular individual, and a table of his fortunes throughout life
To receive (one's) destined award, to meet (one's) fate
adj. (f. -ī), 'Burned or spoiled from birth,' originally bad; unfortunate, wretched; — very little
janm-ā-janam, adv. From birth to birth, one life after another; in successive transmigrations
s.m. = janma-da, janmad, s.m. 'Birth-giver'; father, progenitor; — the Author of existence
adj. & s.m. Sickly from birth; — a valetudinarian
s.m. A fortunate or well-spent life
s.f. A short horoscope; a generalogical table
s.m. 'Pillar or stay of birth'; an epithet of Vishnu
janam-lagan, s.m. The conjunction of the sun and a sign of the Ecliptic at the moment of a birth; the moment of birth; a horoscope, &c. ( = janam-pattrī)
v.n. To become incarnate (as a deity); to be born; to transmigrate (a soul)
(janma+ut˚), s.m. A festival held on the anniversary of Kr̤ishṇa's birthday.
ex: جَدِید نَثْر اُرْدُو کا جَنَم فورْٹ وِلّیَم کالِج کَلْکَتَّہ میں ہُوا
jadīd nasr urdū kā janam forṭ villyam kālij kalkatta mẽ huā
The birth of modern Urdu prose occurred in Fort William College, Calcutta

def: v.t. (caus. of janamnā), To beget; to cause to vegetate.
s.m. = janwāsā, q.v. s.m. The place at the bride's house where the bridegroom and his train are received.
s.m. A living being, a man, a creature, an animal; — adj. Innate, inborn; constitutional, inherited, natural; — impotent from birth.
s.m. = janmī, q.v. s.m. A living being, a man, a creature, an animal; — adj. Innate, inborn; constitutional, inherited, natural; — impotent from birth.
s.m. See s.v. janm.
s.m. The father of the jinn or genii; the genii (collectively), the race of the jinn; a jinn.

ety: jān˚ = Prk. जाण(इ) = S. जाना(ति), rt. ज्ञा;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: s.m. Knowledge, cognizance, apprehension, opinion, &c. (e.g. hamāre jānne-meṅ āʼī hai)
subs: (-ko), To become known or clear, to be evident; to be perceived, to be visible, to appear; to seem, to occur (mentally, to); to approve itself (to)
(-ko), To bear in mind, to remember
v.t. To get a knowledge of, to understand, perceive, apprehend; to recognize.

def: v.n. To be born; to be delivered (of a child), to give birth (to); — v.t. To bring forth, produce; to give birth to; to cast young; to generate, beget, breed.

s.m. = jāta, jāt, q.v.; — s.f. = jātā, q.v. part. Born, brought into existence, brought forth, engendered, grown, produced; — s.m. Male issue, a son
